title: Odontites squarrosus
taxonomy: Odontites squarrosus is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae, commonly known as broomrapes. It is a parasitic plant, meaning it obtains its nutrients by connecting to the roots of other plants. The genus Odontites belongs to the order Lamiales, which includes other flowering plants such as mint and lavender. Odontites squarrosus is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa. It is a perennial plant with small white or purple flowers.

intro: Odontites squarrosus, also known as Square-stalked Toothwort or Square-stalked Downy-toothwort, is a perennial plant belonging to the borage family (Boraginaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including respiratory problems and inflammation, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Odontites squarrosus include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, Odontites squarrosus is often prepared as a tincture or infused in tea, and is also used in the production of topical creams and ointments for skin conditions.
